tagline: Senior Frontend Engineer & Product UI Architect
cover:
I'm excited to apply for the Senior Frontend Engineer role on the Frame.io team. With 12+ years building scalable web applications & frontend architecture at Adobe & Red Hat—and deep familiarity with Adobe's culture, teams, & technical landscape—I'm eager to bring my product engineering expertise to Frame.io's flagship product.

As Staff Design Engineer on Spectrum, I shipped polished, accessible UI that powers Adobe's flagship products including Photoshop, Illustrator, & Express. I built higher-level web components by composing base components into larger patterns, ensuring pixel-perfect design fidelity & WCAG AA compliance across every implementation. I migrated documentation & prototyping to Storybook, architected modern build tooling to reduce technical debt, & drove cross-functional collaboration with design, product, & accessibility experts. My work on the Spectrum AI Working Group—building workflows that converted engineer descriptions into sprint-ready tickets—demonstrated my focus on shipping velocity & developer productivity.

Before Spectrum, I served as Senior Web Components Developer on the Photoshop team, where I initialized & led the Unified Experience Components project serving all Adobe web products. I built semantic, performant components with keyboard navigation & high-contrast mode as requirements, defined best practices for pattern building, & facilitated alignment between application teams & design—exactly the kind of cross-functional product work Frame.io needs.

At Red Hat, I co-founded PatternFly Elements & led frontend architecture across enterprise applications, building component theming systems, visual regression testing with Mocha/Chai & WebdriverIO, & expert-level Sass/CSS architecture. I collaborated with Google, IBM, Fidelity, & Penn State on shared standards & drove adoption through agile, iterative delivery.

Returning to Adobe on the Frame.io team would let me focus deeply on what I love most: shipping high-quality, performant frontend features in close collaboration with design & product. I'd welcome the chance to discuss how my experience can contribute to Frame.io's next chapter.
about: Senior Frontend Engineer with 12+ years shipping scalable, accessible web applications at Adobe & Red Hat. Former Staff Design Engineer on Adobe Spectrum & Senior Web Components Developer on Photoshop. Deep expertise in TypeScript, web components, CSS architecture, Storybook, & building pixel-perfect, performant UI. W3C CSS Working Group member with proven ability to collaborate cross-functionally with design, product, & QA to shape, prototype, & ship features that millions rely on.

items: Cross-functional collaboration with design, product, & QA

Advocating for accessibility standards & helping teams implement WCAG 2.2 AA compliance
Breaking down complex frontend challenges into actionable decisions with clearly identified tradeoffs
Publishing detailed guides & documentation that codify best practices for frontend development

highlights: Built higher-level web components by composing base components into larger patterns, ensuring pixel-perfect design fidelity & WCAG AA compliance
